examples: Economists argue ferociously about how close this is to being true.
He clenched his fists tightly and shook them ferociously at Miss Martha.
Over the past weeks, we've seen Arab people ferociously attached to their national identities.
Both were out on a limb, ferociously challenging the slovenly relativism of everyone else.
The blame lies with the four big rivals of Ivorian politics, who squabble ferociously.
